text:
Urban traffic congestion remains a critical global challenge, contributing to economic loss, environmental degradation, and delayed emergency responses. Traditional traffic control systems typically rely on fixed-logic scheduling, which fails to adapt to real-time traffic fluctuations. While recent IoT-based proposals attempt to estimate traffic density using deep learning, they often struggle with the accurate identification of emergency vehicles due to the scarcity and low quality of specialized image datasets. 

This paper introduces a multi-modal distributed IoT framework designed to enhance urban traffic management through two key innovations. First, observing that road infrastructure remains largely static, we propose a novel two-stage vehicle detector, "R-YOLO." This system utilizes a road-masking technique to constrain the search space, achieving a vehicle detection accuracy of 77% (a significant improvement over existing end-to-end models) while maintaining the low latency required for edge deployment on devices like Raspberry Pi. Second, to address the limitations of visual detection for emergency priority, we implement an acoustic detection algorithm using a sliding window approach to identify the unique siren frequencies of emergency vehicles. Experimental results demonstrate that this acoustic system achieves an average detection accuracy of 99.4%. By integrating vision and sound, the proposed distributed system provides a resilient and cost-effective solution for real-time intelligent traffic control.
